review_notes: Holdout packet surfaces `httpd` as the top candidate, but the evidence still lacks handler-surface proof and a concrete attacker-controlled command construction path. Keep this family conservative until direct binary review confirms the `foreign_share_file_system()` chain.

Same RT-AX58U packet structure as 25127: strong web stack visibility but no demonstrated request-to-command chain beyond the `httpd` triage summary. Treat this as a known mismatch candidate, not yet as confirmed command injection.
Same RT-AX58U family again: current packet is enough to keep the sample in rootfs-success, but not enough to elevate it to command injection without direct reversing of the `httpd` handler path.

Even though extraction remains blob-level, the strongest repeated signals in the packet are overflow-style library findings (`libdiag.so`, `libtirpc.so`, `liblbcmnlibs.so`) rather than command execution. Keep artifact state conservative but classify the leading risk family as memory-corruption.

Current holdout packet exposes only generic signed-blob signals with no extracted web handlers or candidate dossier evidence. Keep this as container/blob analysis work rather than speculative vulnerability triage.
Same as MX4200: signed blob delivery with generic blob evidence only. No basis yet to claim a stronger risk family than container-analysis.

The holdout packet shows a clean rootfs extraction but no surfaced candidates, no web-exposed chains, and no stronger structural issue class. Keep this as successful extraction with conservative `no-clear-rce` triage rather than forcing a stronger family.
